title: Tropical Crisp Brazilian Chicken Fried Rice
description: A floral and crisp Brazilian dish featuring oyster sauce and dairy-free friendly ingredients, perfect for meal prep. This recipe combines the flavors of Brazil with the convenience of a one-pan meal.
instructions: Heat the coconut oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken to the skillet and cook until browned and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes.
Remove the chicken from the skillet and set aside.
Add the diced onion to the skillet and cook until translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Add the frozen peas and carrots to the skillet and cook until thawed and tender.
Add the cooked rice to the skillet, breaking up any clumps with a spatula.
Stir in the oyster sauce, salt, and black pepper, and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes.
Return the cooked chicken to the skillet and stir to combine with the rice mixture.
Cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until the chicken is heated through and the rice is crispy.
